prompt_text:
You are shown a SKELETON view of a java repository: class headers,
inheritance, method signatures, and attributes — but NO method bodies.

Use this signature-level view to detect structural violations of Single Responsibility Principle (SRP):
- Fat interfaces / bloated classes (many unrelated method groups)
- Clients forced to implement methods they don't need (abstract methods / NotImplementedError / empty overrides)
- Inheritance hierarchies where subclasses alter the contract shape
- Broad AP...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/execution/AbstractPipelineExt.java",
      "entity_name": "AbstractPipelineExt",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 119,
      "line_end": 950,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Bloated class handling pipeline execution, metrics collection, and DLQ management.",
      "reasoning": "This class encapsulates multiple unrelated responsibilities: pipeline lifecycle management, complex metrics initialization and storage (L546-L780), Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) writer lifecycle (L387-L458), and utility logic like time duration parsing (L420). Changes to the metrics system or DLQ implementation would force changes to this core pipeline class."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 55,
      "line_end": 980,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "God class managing queue logic, disk I/O, and resource monitoring.",
      "reasoning": "The Queue class handles high-level queue operations (read/write/ack), low-level page management (L185-L211), file locking/concurrency (L204, L823), serialization (L781), and physical disk space monitoring (L917). These represent separate concerns (logic, persistence, and resource management) that should be decoupled."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/Event.java",
      "entity_name": "Event",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 51,
      "line_end": 583,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Data entity class mixed with serialization and formatting logic.",
      "reasoning": "While primarily a data container, the class also assumes responsibility for JSON parsing/serialization (L284-L314), binary serialization (L541), memory estimation (L577), and complex string interpolation (L397). Serialization formats and data structure management are distinct reasons for change."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/io/DeadLetterQueueWriter.java",
      "entity_name": "DeadLetterQueueWriter",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 109,
      "line_end": 814,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Class mixing write operations with retention policy management and scheduling.",
      "reasoning": "The class is responsible for writing entries to the DLQ, but also contains logic for age-based and size-based retention policies (L458, L489), segment cleanup, file locking, and managing its own background flusher via an internal scheduler service. Retention management is a separate administrative concern from the writer's primary task."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/lib/logstash/agent.rb",
      "entity_name": "LogStash",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 47,
      "line_end": 195,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Ruby Agent class acting as a God Object for the entire system.",
      "reasoning": "This class (and its associated top-level functions) manages pipeline lifecycles, webserver initialization (L481), metric collector configuration (L510), state convergence logic (L382), and geoip database metrics (L660). It ties together too many unrelated subsystems."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/config/ir/compiler/DatasetCompiler.java",
      "entity_name": "DatasetCompiler",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 41,
      "line_end": 574,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Compiler class mixed with runtime dataset implementations.",
      "reasoning": "The class contains the logic to compile datasets but also defines internal runtime structures like 'Complement' (L486) and 'ComputeAndClear' (L547). The logic for generating/compiling execution code should be separated from the operational behavior of the resulting dataset objects."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Bloated class handling pipeline execution, metrics collection, and DLQ management.
findings.reasoning: This class encapsulates multiple unrelated responsibilities: pipeline lifecycle management, complex metrics initialization and storage (L546-L780), Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) writer lifecycle (L387-L458), and utility logic like time duration parsing (L420). Changes to the metrics system or DLQ implementation would force changes to this core pipeline class.

findings.description: God class managing queue logic, disk I/O, and resource monitoring.
findings.reasoning: The Queue class handles high-level queue operations (read/write/ack), low-level page management (L185-L211), file locking/concurrency (L204, L823), serialization (L781), and physical disk space monitoring (L917). These represent separate concerns (logic, persistence, and resource management) that should be decoupled.

findings.description: Data entity class mixed with serialization and formatting logic.
findings.reasoning: While primarily a data container, the class also assumes responsibility for JSON parsing/serialization (L284-L314), binary serialization (L541), memory estimation (L577), and complex string interpolation (L397). Serialization formats and data structure management are distinct reasons for change.

findings.description: Class mixing write operations with retention policy management and scheduling.
findings.reasoning: The class is responsible for writing entries to the DLQ, but also contains logic for age-based and size-based retention policies (L458, L489), segment cleanup, file locking, and managing its own background flusher via an internal scheduler service. Retention management is a separate administrative concern from the writer's primary task.

findings.description: Ruby Agent class acting as a God Object for the entire system.
findings.reasoning: This class (and its associated top-level functions) manages pipeline lifecycles, webserver initialization (L481), metric collector configuration (L510), state convergence logic (L382), and geoip database metrics (L660). It ties together too many unrelated subsystems.

findings.description: Compiler class mixed with runtime dataset implementations.
findings.reasoning: The class contains the logic to compile datasets but also defines internal runtime structures like 'Complement' (L486) and 'ComputeAndClear' (L547). The logic for generating/compiling execution code should be separated from the operational behavior of the resulting dataset objects.
